resources.string:
Couldn't enable monitor mode on %1$s.

Why this happens:
• The adapter or its chipset doesn't support monitor mode (most built-in non-Qualcomm Wi-Fi chips don't).
• The adapter isn't detected or was unplugged.
• The interface is busy or down, or the driver exposes it under a different name.

How to fix:
• Settings → Network interfaces: pick a different adapter for handshake/deauth.
• Re-plug your external USB Wi-Fi adapter, then rescan.
• Use a monitor-capable adapter (e.g. Atheros AR9271, Realtek RTL8812AU / 88XXAU).
• Settings → Custom monitor commands: adjust how monitor mode is enabled for your adapter.
• Make sure Android Wi-Fi / hotspot isn't holding the interface — toggle it off and try again.
